title: Viral RNA Extraction and Detection for Enterovirus
author: Min Chen
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.jhzcj76

steps.instruction: 1.Stool suspensions were created by mixing 0.1 g of stool sample, 0.1 mL of chloroform, and 1 mL of phosphate-buffered saline.

steps.title: The suspensions were shaken vigorously for...
steps.instruction: The suspensions were shaken vigorously for 20 min followed by centrifugation at 3,000 × g for 5 min at 4°C.
3.For rectal swab samples, the supernatant was prepared by centrifuging the fluid at 13,000 × g for 1 min and transferring the supernatant to a fresh tube.

steps.title: Viral RNA was extracted from 200...
steps.instruction: Viral RNA was extracted from 200 μL of supernatant using the viral RNA mini kit (Qiagen, Hilden, Germany) according to the manufacturer’s instructions.
